signIn.desc: Sign in to keep your library, wishlist, and reading plans synced across all your devices.
signIn.guestPrompt: Prefer to try it offline first? You can also {link} (your data will live in this browser only).

about.title: About the {accent}
about.subtitle: Why this exists, what it does, and where it's going.
about.originBody1: Most readers I know have an Amazon wishlist, a Goodreads pile, a Notes app full of titles, a stack on the nightstand. Hundreds of books in queue — and somehow, every Friday night, we still spend twenty minutes deciding what to read next.
about.originBody2: The Oracle started as a personal experiment to fix that. The premise: a wishlist shouldn't just be a hoarding pile. It should be the input to a recommendation engine that already knows your taste, because you built it yourself.
about.originBody3: That experiment grew into something larger — a full reading companion with clubs, shared sessions, progress tracking, and an AI that doesn't just suggest books but helps your group decide what to read together.
about.evolutionItem1Title: v1–v2 — HTML batches and vanilla JS
about.evolutionItem1Body: Instagram screenshots of book recommendations became styled HTML pages. Then a single-page app in plain JavaScript with localStorage — the first Oracle category and similarity matching. Worked, but didn't scale past one device.
about.evolutionItem2Title: v3–v25 — React, sync, and the reading stack
about.evolutionItem2Body: React with Vite, Supabase for cross-device sync, Google authentication. The Oracle grew to two modes (category and similarity), AI-powered reading plans arrived, and the full reading lifecycle took shape: Wishlist → Read Next → Currently Reading → Library.
about.evolutionItem3Body: Book Clubs launched the app's first multi-user feature: shared reading groups, sessions tied to a specific book, real-time member progress tracking. Discussion threads, admin-pinned questions, Oracle-powered conversation starters, and group polls for deciding what to read next followed in the next release.
about.featuresIntro: Each feature is here to answer a different version of "what should I read next?" — alone or with others.
about.featureWishlistBody: Your full pile of books you intend to read someday. Pre-seeded from a curated catalog of over a thousand titles, but fully editable. Books leave the wishlist automatically when you mark them as read. Bulk Goodreads CSV import available from your Profile.
about.featureLibraryBody: Everything you've finished, with star ratings, optional notes, and read dates. The Oracle draws on your library to understand your taste. Filter by genre, sort by rating or date, browse by series.
about.featureReadNextBody: A short queue — the books you've actually committed to reading next. Different from the wishlist, which is aspirational. Drag to reorder, move to Currently Reading in one tap.
about.featureCurrentlyReadingBody: Track books actively in progress with a start date and page-level progress. A live progress bar shows how far through each book you are. Finish a book from here and it moves to your Library with your rating.
about.featureOracleBody: The recommendation engine, with two ways to draw books from your vault:
about.featureOracleCategoriesBody: Pick a temperament — folk horror, gothic, Latin American literary fiction, cozy fantasy — and the Oracle draws books for you to choose from.
about.featureOracleSimilarBody: Tell the Oracle one to three books you've loved. It finds others with the same blood in them.
about.featureOracleModes: Both modes work in two ways: Vault-only draws only from your wishlist (fast, deterministic). AI-powered calls Claude and may surface books beyond your vault, with reasoning.
about.featurePlansBody: A reading plan is a small, structured commitment: "finish this series," "read three books on grief," "work through this author's catalog." Plans are AI-generated from a prompt, ordered by the Oracle, and shareable via URL. Multiple active plans are supported.
about.featureListsBody: Create curated reading lists from any books in your collection — a genre deep-dive, a gift recommendation, a themed shelf. Lists can be made public and shared via a link that renders beautifully for anyone, no account needed.
about.featureClubsBody: Create a reading group, invite members via a private join link, and read together. Admins create Sessions — each tied to one book with a date range and notes for the group. The session page shows every member's live reading progress against the book.
about.featureDiscussionBody: Sessions have full discussion threads: free comments, admin-pinned questions with their own answer threads, and one level of replies. Tap "☩ Oracle suggests" for AI-generated discussion questions tailored to the book. Club polls let the group vote on what to read next — or let the Oracle suggest three candidates and turn them into a poll automatically.
about.featureDashboardBody: Your home screen. Currently Reading books front and center, active Book Club sessions at a glance, all reading plans below, and a paginated activity feed showing your recent reading history.
about.roadmapBody: The Oracle isn't trying to become Goodreads. The destination is something more opinionated: a reading companion that knows your taste deeply, helps your group read together, and uses AI where it actually adds value — not everywhere.
about.feedbackBody: This is a prototype with real testers, and feedback shapes what gets built next. If something feels off, missing, or genuinely useful — say so.
about.roadmapTierNear1Body: Follow other readers, see their finished books and clubs in your dashboard feed. Public profiles with reading stats and current reads.
about.roadmapTierNear2Body: Generate a styled image card for Instagram, X, or WhatsApp — "just finished Piranesi ★★★★★" or "our club is reading Middlemarch this month." Dark-academia aesthetic, exports as PNG.
about.roadmapTierNear3Body: When a session ends, the Oracle generates a short summary of the group discussion, overall sentiment on the book, and a suggested next read. Shareable as a page.
about.roadmapTierMed1Body: Clubs that opt in appear in a browsable genre-filtered directory. Keeps private clubs hidden; opens the app to new readers discovering groups to join.
about.roadmapTierMed2Body: Annual "read N books" goals with a dashboard progress bar. Clubs can run their own seasonal challenges.
about.roadmapTierMed3Body: Better duplicate detection, improved series handling, and progress feedback for large libraries (500+ books).
about.roadmapTierFuture1Body: Oracle-generated entry points into genres — "Start here for Gothic horror" — built from the series and genre data already in the catalog.
about.roadmapTierFuture2Body: Oracle prompts in Spanish for Spanish-language books, significantly improving data quality for Latin American titles.
about.roadmapTierFuture3Body: Personal annotations tied to specific pages or chapters, private by default, optionally shared with your club.
